channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.
channel.copyright: Copyright:(C) Sat, 30 May 2026 09:05:55 IST Bennett Coleman & Co. Ltd, http://in.indiatimes.com/policyterms/1554651.cms

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: Gulf's other war: UAE carried out strikes on Iran despite ceasefire — Report
item.description: The UAE's involvement in the Middle East conflict was deeper than previously known, with coordinated strikes on Iran, including energy facilities, even after an April ceasefire. These actions exposed divisions within the Gulf, as Saudi Arabia expressed concerns about escalating risks to regional energy supplies and global markets.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'They walk among us': White House launches alien-themed website to track immigrants
item.description: The White House launched a new space-themed website, Aliens.gov, to highlight immigration enforcement actions. Using extraterrestrial imagery, the site aims to showcase arrests of undocumented immigrants and promote President Trump's agenda. It features a live dashboard and interactive map of enforcement activities, drawing criticism for its dehumanizing language.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: China building launch pads near nuclear missile silos
Is IShowSpeed working on a FIFA World Cup 2026 song? Behind-the-scenes footage sparks major fan speculation
item.description: Behind-the-scenes footage has sparked speculation that IShowSpeed may be working on a FIFA World Cup 2026 song. Videos from a large-scale shoot in Miami showed the streamer surrounded by production crews, drones, flags, and football-themed visuals. Fans linked the project to the upcoming tournament, especially after Speed hinted at World Cup plans during a recent tour. The rumors have gained traction because his 2022 World Cup anthem became a viral hit, amassing more than 208 million views on YouTube.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: "Your dead husband....": Candace Owens slams Erika Kirk over Charlie Kirk’s private letters, calls fundraising effort “shameless”
item.description: Candace Owens sparked a heated online debate after criticizing Erika Kirk for sharing personal letters and notes written by her late husband, Charlie Kirk, as part of fundraising efforts. Owens called the move “tasteless” and accused Kirk of using grief for financial gain. While critics agreed that private correspondence should remain private, supporters argued the posts were a heartfelt tribute that honored Charlie Kirk’s memory and offered comfort to others experiencing loss.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: DJ Akademiks spots bigger problem in Clavicular and DaBaby nightclub confrontation caught on video
item.description: A disagreement between Clavicular and DaBaby during a May 27 performance at Bacara nightclub in Miami went viral online. Clavicular claimed members of DaBaby’s team violated the club’s no-firearm policy, while DaBaby continued performing. DJ Akademiks later reacted on Rumble, warning Clavicular about confronting the rapper and adding more attention to the incident.
item.dc:creator: Sehjal Gupta

item.title: “No dignity”: Matt Walsh erupts over Erika Kirk boyfriend rumor while Blake Wynn breaks silence
item.description: Matt Walsh criticized an X user for spreading claims that Erika Kirk was dating Blake Wynn. The rumor originated from Project Constitution, which alleged the two were seen together at a Beverly Hills hotel. Erika Kirk strongly denied the accusation, saying she was in Arizona with her family. Blake Wynn also rejected the rumor and said they are not dating.
item.dc:creator: Sehjal Gupta

item.title: “This is getting bad guys”: Twitch streamer Asmongold reflects on being 36, single, and isolated
item.description: Popular streamer Asmongold shared his feelings of loneliness and a monotonous daily life. The 36-year-old content creator spoke about his solitary existence and repetitive chores. His candid admission resonated with many viewers online. The viral video has sparked conversations about isolation and mental well-being among internet personalities. Asmongold's honesty highlights struggles faced by successful online figures.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Pregnant woman forced to sign deportation papers after being held at US airport for days
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: YouTuber sued by Ontario Tech University after allegedly throwing curry at students during prank
item.description: Ontario Tech University is suing YouTuber Fique Ayub for allegedly disrupting classes and throwing curry at students during prank videos filmed on campus. The university claims Ayub entered lecture halls without permission, cooked curry, and initiated a "catch the curry" game, causing damage and distress. Ayub denies the allegations, stating his videos are exaggerated comedy and satire.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: 'If you do it badly...': JPMorgan CEO confronts Mamdani during closed-door meeting
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Meet Tesh Jennings, an Indian-American rancher who says 'not all Indians code'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: US judge orders removal of Donald Trump's name from Kennedy Center, blocks closure plan
item.description: A US federal judge has ordered the Trump administration to remove President Trump's name from the Kennedy Center. The court ruled that renaming the Washington institution, a memorial to John F. Kennedy, requires congressional approval. The judge mandated the removal of all Trump signage and references within two weeks, following a board decision that faced significant backlash.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: H-1B Redditor says he applied to 1500+ job openings across US, Internet says 'go back home'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Pam Bondi says Ghislaine Maxwell should ‘die in prison’ amid Epstein files investigation
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Euripides: “For in other ways a woman is full of fear, defenseless, dreads the sight of cold steel; but, when once she…”
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Who is Allen Ferrell? YouTuber banned for life from Cedar Point and all Six Flags parks over chicken nugget stunt
item.description: YouTuber Allen Ferrell has been banned for life from all Six Flags parks after filming himself eating chicken nuggets on Cedar Point’s Millennium Force roller coaster in Ohio. Park officials said the stunt violated safety rules that ban loose items on rides. Ferrell called it a fun challenge, but Six Flags said safety concerns left no room for exceptions.
item.dc:creator: Sehjal Gupta

item.title: Meet the Moon Trees: Apollo 14 astronaut carried hundreds of tree seeds around the Moon in 1971 and now they are flourishing across America
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: The ghost in the machine is real: How physicists finally tracked down the hidden force disrupting CERN's most powerful accelerator
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Canada dips into technical recession? Pierre Poilievre slams Mark Carney, says no other G7 country is in this situation
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
